rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into component implementations only when bindings depend on subcomponent-level details.
Preserve AADL structure by not altering component types, connections, or subcomponents during annex insertion.
generic_rules: Place component-level assumptions/guarantees in type scopes; implementation-specific bindings in implementation scopes.
Only reference features, ports, or data types explicitly defined and visible in the AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` for all AGREE contracts, with no external syntax or inline annotations.
Ensure all variables in AGREE expressions correspond to AADL ports or declared constants, not properties or literals.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce AGREE clauses that reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they were variables.
Avoid using subcomponent paths or internal implementation details in component type annexes.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE assign statements and fusion logic
